characters.name: Alan Cornwall
characters.description: Alan CornwallBackstagePortrayed byNicholas LyndhurstFirst seenThe Good FatherEpisodes10Dr. Alan Cornwallis a character from theFrasier2023 reboot. He is portrayed byNicholas Lyndhurst. Alan Cornwall is an old college classmate of Frasier Crane and Harvard psychology professor. Despite his occupation, he does not appear to have any affection for his students. The reason for this is currently unknown. Alan and Frasier's friendship dates back to their Oxford days. While there, they both tried to join the private Bullingdon Club. However, after getting lights-out drunk, they snuck into the library and tried to steal Oscar Wilde's walking stick, but ended up getting tackled by a pack of surprisingly spry librarians and forever banned from the club. Alan Cornwall is an old college classmate of Frasier Crane and Harvard psychology professor. Despite his occupation, he does not appear to have any affection for his students. The reason for this is currently unknown.

characters.name: Alice Doyle
characters.description: Alice DoyleBornAlice May DoyleBirthdayMay 1998FromSeattle, WashingtonResidenceRoz's ApartmentRelationshipsFamilyRoz Doyle(mother)Rick Garrett(biological father)Joanna Doyle(maternal grandmother)Paula Garrett(paternal grandmother)Steve Garrett(paternal grandfather)Denise Doyle(maternal aunt)BackstagePortrayed byAshley ThomasGreer GrammerSeason(s)5•6•7•8•9•10•112(2023)First seenLast seenThe Life Of The PartyFather ChristmasEpisodes23(1993) •2(2023)Alice Doyleis a recurring character onFrasier, portrayed chiefly by Ashley Thomas. She joined the second season of the reboot, now portrayed by Greer Grammer.[1] The only daughter ofRoz Doyle, Alice is born May 1998, after Roz goes into labor at a party atNiles Crane’sapartment[2], where the pregnancy was also revealed. Alice is born as a result of a one-night stand with a20-year old college student. Her father is only 20 when she is conceived, and rather than, as Roz thought of it, ruin his life, Roz decides to raise Alice herself and not burden him with the responsibility.

characters.name: Bebe Glazer
characters.description: Bebe Glazer2023ClassicOccupationTalent AgentRelationshipsFamilyPhoebe GlazerUnnamed sonRomancesFrasier Crane(fling)BackstagePortrayed byHarriet Sansom HarrisFirst seenLast seenSelling OutGoodnight, Seattle: Part 2Episodes11 Unnamed son Bebe Glazeris a recurring character onFrasier, portrayed byHarriet Sansom Harris. She is highly manipulated talent agent who works for Fraiser. Fraiser and Niles despise her due to her lack of ethics and always being shameless as she always think of making best deals for her and her client, regardless of morals or ethics. Bebe is a Seattle talent agent who representsFrasier. Bebe Glazer used to be the agent ofBulldog. Bebe is essentially the seriesMephistopheles, repeatedly offering Frasier all sorts of material advantages if he will compromise his ethical standards, although her name perhaps leans more towardsBeelzebub. She is described as a vampire; rosemary's baby; or a demon. Fraiser claims that even Satan worships her and phone number is 666.Niles Cranedeclares that she is "Lady Macbethwithout the sincerity". ("Agents In America: Part 3")

characters.name: Bernard Schenkman
characters.description: Bernard SchenkmanOccupationMarriage counselorRelationshipsRomancesMaris CraneBackstagePortrayed byBob DishySeason(s)Season 5Only seenThe Maris CounselorDr. Bernard Schenkmanis a character onFrasier, portrayed by Bob Dishy. In 1995,NilesandMarisseparate. By 1997, Niles is no longer willing to stay in the limbo and tells Maris that either they go into couples counseling or divorce.Maris agrees, but becomes dissatisfied with all of their therapists, and fires many of them, including Dr. Deutsch[1]. In 1998, having just fired Dr. Prescott, she also wants to fire Dr. Wilphaum, claiming his criticism of her is too harsh after he asked her to refrain from catalogue shopping during the sessions, but on advice fromFrasier, Niles decides to tell her that they won't change therapists again,resulting in her withholding sex from him,so Niles eventually relents, and hires Dr. Bernard Schenkman. After Schenkman told Niles that his problem is that he's too predictable, Niles decides to surprise Maris with a romantic evening. When he's doing the preparations, Schenkman comes in and brings a bottle of champagne. However, he and Niles stay out of each others' sights and when Niles sees the champagne bottle, he thinks Maris brought it. Niles then lays down in the bed and turns off the bedroom light. Schenkman then comes out of the bathroom and also gets in bed. They both reach for each other, asking "Maris?" at the same time, then start screaming. Niles then turns on the bedroom light and they scream again.

characters.name: Bernice Brisco
characters.description: Bernice BriscoRelationshipsFamilyBob Brisco(son)BackstagePortrayed byPeggy MileySeason(s)Season 5Only seenBad DogBernice Briscoeis a character onFrasier, portrayed by Peggy Miley. Bernice is the mother of radio personalityBulldog. Bulldog mentions her from time to time and she attends the 1998 SeaBee Awards ceremony, where her son wins a special heroism award. However, Bulldog later uses her as a shield, a treachery which she repays by belting him around the head.[1]Bulldog at one point paid her to pretend that she was in the hospital soFrasier Cranewould take his evening slot.

characters.name: Billy Moon
characters.description: William MoonOccupationBallroom Dance InstructorRelationshipsFamilyDaphne Moon(sister)Gertrude Moon(mother)Harry Moon(father)RomancesKevinBackstageWilliam "Billy" Moonis a character onFrasier. He is one of ofDaphne's eight brothers, along withSimon,Stephen,Nigel,Michael,Peter,DavidandReginald. Billy is one of Daphne's many brothers and is the sole of the Moon sons not to work at the docks. He is matriarchGertrude'sfavourite son and teaches ballroom dancing.[1]Daphne mentions how Billy has never peeked on her while she was undressing or showering, as all the others did during their years of pubescence. Billy did peek at Daphne’s other brother, Nigel, though. Later, their motherGertrudeadvicesDaphneto seduceher husbandusing her feminine wiles, as "that's how your brother Billy landed Kevin". However, Daphne remarks that "sharing a cell helped".

characters.name: Blaine Sternin
characters.description: Blaine SterninOccupationCon manRelationshipsFamilyLilith Sternin(sister)Frederick Crane(nephew)BackstagePortrayed byMichael KeatonOnly seenWheels of FortuneBlaine Sterninis a guest character on NBC seriesFrasier. He is portrayed byMichael Keaton. Blaine isLilith's half-brother, the black sheep of the family (Lilith being "the good one"), and has a sizeable, substantiated reputation as a con artist. Prior to his appearance inFrasier, he had tricked money out of people in Maine using the alias "Brad Cunningham", in Louisiana as "Royce Thibideaux" and in Albuquerque, New Mexico as "Santana Del La Cruz". He also sold forged autographs of famous people at one time; a wheeze which ended when it was discovered that his Marie Curie and Sugar Ray Robinson signatures had the same handwriting. He also fenced fake handicap plates. Blaine appears to have connedFrasierseveral times; he sold him a fake deed for a 50,000-acre kelp farm, and once stole an antique salt server. He was said to have once posed as Frasier for a year, during which time he "treated" some of the latter's patients. characters.name: Brian Patchett
characters.description: Brian PatchettResidenceBoston, MassachusettsEducationMassachusetts Institute of TechnologyOccupationSeismologist, Massachusetts Institute of TechnologyRelationshipsFamilyFrederick Crane(former step-son)RomancesStan Dublowsky(boyfriend)Lilith Sternin(ex-wife)BackstagePortrayed byJames MorrisonSeason(s)Season 2Only seenAdventures In Paradise: Part 2Brian Patchettis a guest character inSeason 2ofFrasier, portrayed by James Morrison. Brian is introduced in episode "Adventures in Paradise: Part 2", when Frasier has taken his date Madeline Marshall on a romantic getaway to Bora Bora. Much to his displeasure, they discover that at their hotel, Frasier's ex-wifeLilith Sterninis staying right in the next room with her new beau, Brian, a MIT seismologist. At the end of the episode, Lilith visits Frasier in Seattle, telling him that she and Brian are getting married. However, 3.5 years later, Lilith comes to visit Frasier again in Seattle, and reveals after she and Brian had hired contractor Stan Jablonski to extend their master bedroom, Brian ended up coming out as gay and left her for Stan.

characters.name: Bridget
characters.description: BridgetRelationshipsRomancesDonny DouglasBackstagePortrayed byTwink CaplanSeason(s)Season 10Only seenThe Ring CycleBridgetis a guest character inSeason 10ofFrasier, portrayed by Twink Caplan. Bridget is the fiancée of Donny Douglas and meets everyone at the courthouse where Daphne and Niles are getting married. Donny thanks Daphne, for if she had not left him at the altar for Niles, he would not have met his ex-wife once more. This is part of what spurs Daphne and Niles to take the immediate plunge also.[1]

characters.name: Charlotte Connor
characters.description: Charlotte ConnorBirthday1969ResidenceChicago, ILOccupationMatchmakerRelationshipsFamilyUnnamed Lady(mother)RomancesFrasier CraneFrank(ex-boyfriend)Unnamed man(ex-husband)BackstagePortrayed byLaura LinneySeason(s)Season 11First seenLast seenMatch GameGoodnight, Seattle: Part 1Episodes6Charlotte Connoris a recurring character inSeason 11ofFrasier. She is portrayed byLaura Linney. Charlotte is a savvy, smart, charming matchmaker, whomFrasierseeks out to find a good partner. Charlotte and Frasier meet when Frasier is looking to seriously improve his romantic prospects, as his his brother is having a baby and his father is getting remarried, making him feel lonely and empty. After charging Frasier $10,000, Charlotte sends him on a series of bad dates, causing Frasier to lose patience and demand his money back. He soon discovers that Charlotte only has five clients in her match making service, and scammed him out of ten thousand dollars with her illegitimate business run out of her apartment. In the end, Frasier forgives her and finds himself falling for her, only to discover that she has a boyfriend.[1] Charlotte is a savvy, smart, charming matchmaker, whomFrasierseeks out to find a good partner.
characters.personality_section: Charlotte is a savvy, smart, charming matchmaker, whomFrasierseeks out to find a good partner.

characters.name: Clint Weber
characters.description: Clint WeberBornClint WeberOccupationHealth Chat host atKACLBackstagePortrayed byBilly CampbellSeason(s)Season 5Only seenThe Perfect GuyDr. Clint Weberis a character on NBC seriesFrasier.He is played by Billy Campbell. Dr. Weber is the host of the KACL show, Health Chat. He is only seen in one episode, "The Perfect Guy". He comes off as being "perfect", and in almost all ways Frasier's superior. In addition to being charming, strikingly handsome and very intelligent, he attended Oxford University, and is an apparently excellent diagnostician. He also received a Master's in French History. Putting himself through school by working as a sous-chef in Paris, he is a terrific cook, and a new cocktail he mixes at Frasier's party, the "Pink Webber", is an instant hit. He is a godson of the Three Tenor's famous Jose Carerras, fluently speaks both French and Mandarin Chinese, and was able to predict the attack pattern of a Russian Grandmaster's long-distance chess game against Frasier after only a moment of observation. His only failure lies in his off-key singing voice, which Frasier and Niles manipulate to get rid of him. Though it implies that he never worked for the show previously, Bebe talks about his show in "The Zoo Story" a few episodes prior to his introduction. In addition to being charming, strikingly handsome and very intelligent, he attended Oxford University, and is an apparently excellent diagnostician. He also received a Master's in French History. Putting himself through school by working as a sous-chef in Paris, he is a terrific cook, and a new cocktail he mixes at Frasier's party, the "Pink Webber", is an instant hit. He is a godson of the Three Tenor's famous Jose Carerras, fluently speaks both French and Mandarin Chinese, and was able to predict the attack pattern of a Russian Grandmaster's long-distance chess game against Frasier after only a moment of observation. His only failure lies in his off-key singing voice, which Frasier and Niles manipulate to get rid of him. Though it implies that he never worked for the show previously, Bebe talks about his show in "The Zoo Story" a few episodes prior to his introduction.
characters.personality_section: Dr. Weber is the host of the KACL show, Health Chat. He is only seen in one episode, "The Perfect Guy". He comes off as being "perfect", and in almost all ways Frasier's superior. In addition to being charming, strikingly handsome and very intelligent, he attended Oxford University, and is an apparently excellent diagnostician. He also received a Master's in French History. Putting himself through school by working as a sous-chef in Paris, he is a terrific cook, and a new cocktail he mixes at Frasier's party, the "Pink Webber", is an instant hit. He is a godson of the Three Tenor's famous Jose Carerras, fluently speaks both French and Mandarin Chinese, and was able to predict the attack pattern of a Russian Grandmaster's long-distance chess game against Frasier after only a moment of observation. His only failure lies in his off-key singing voice, which Frasier and Niles manipulate to get rid of him. Though it implies that he never worked for the show previously, Bebe talks about his show in "The Zoo Story" a few episodes prior to his introduction.

characters.name: Daphne Moon
characters.description: Daphne MoonBornDaphne MoonBirthdaySeptember 1961AliasDaph • The British Pound(kickboxing)• Stilts(bySimon Moon)• Dappy(from childhood)ResidenceVineyard in Sedona, AZSeattle, WashingtonOccupationPhysical therapistRelationshipsFamilyNiles Crane(husband)David Crane(son)Gertrude Moon(mother)Harry Moon(father)Frasier Crane(brother-in-law)Martin Crane(father-in-law)Ronee Lawrence(stepmother-in-law)Grammy Moon(paternal grandmother)†Stephen Moon(brother)Reginald Moon(brother)David Moon(brother)Peter Moon(brother)Billy Moon(brother)Michael Moon(brother)Nigel Moon(brother)Simon Moon(brother)Hester Crane(mother-in-law)†Frederick Crane(marital nephew)FriendsRoz DoyleHollyAnniePamRomancesNiles Crane(husband)Donny Douglas(ex-fiancé)Greg(dated)Joe DeCarlo(ex-boyfriend)Derek(dated)Eric(dated)Clive(ex-fiancé)BackstagePortrayed byJane LeevesFirst seenLast seenThe Good SonGoodnight, Seattle: Part 2Episodes259Daphne Moonis a principal character onFrasier. She is portrayed byJane Leeves. As the series begins, Daphne is a recent immigrant from England who is employed byFrasier Craneas a live-in housekeeper and physical therapist for his father,Martin Crane. Her quirky personality and claims of psychic abilities as well as abysmal culinary skill provided comic relief on the show, while her relationship with Frasier's brotherNiles Cranewas a major dramatic plotline during the run of the series, progressing from Niles' secret infatuation at the beginning of the show to their marriage at the beginning of season ten. Daphne is hired byFrasier Craneto assist Frasier's father,Martin Crane, with his daily activities and exercises. Martin suffers pain and discomfort from a bullet wound he received two years prior working on the Seattle police force.[1]Incidentally, Daphne, prior to being hired by the Cranes, had a chance encounter with them atCafé Nervosa.[2] As the series begins, Daphne is a recent immigrant from England who is employed byFrasier Craneas a live-in housekeeper and physical therapist for his father,Martin Crane. Her quirky personality and claims of psychic abilities as well as abysmal culinary skill provided comic relief on the show, while her relationship with Frasier's brotherNiles Cranewas a major dramatic plotline during the run of the series, progressing from Niles' secret infatuation at the beginning of the show to their marriage at the beginning of season ten.

characters.name: David Crane
characters.description: David CraneBornDavid CraneBirthday15 May 2004RelationshipsFamilyDaphne Moon(mother)Niles Crane(father)Frasier Crane(paternal uncle)Martin Crane(paternal grandfather)Ronee Lawrence(step-grandmother)Hester Crane(paternal grandmother)†Gertrude Moon(maternal grandmother)Harry Moon(maternal grandfather)Simon Moon(maternal uncle)Reginald Moon(maternal uncle, never seen)David Moon(maternal uncle, never seen)Peter Moon(maternal uncle, never seen)Billy Moon(maternal uncle, never seen)Michael Moon(maternal uncle)Nigel Moon(maternal uncle)Grammy Moon(maternal great-grandmother, never seen)†Stephen Moon(maternal uncle)Frederick Crane(paternal cousin)BackstagePortrayed byAnders KeithSeason(s)11First seenGoodnight, Seattle: Part 1(Frasier)(May 13, 2004)David Craneis the son ofNiles CraneandDaphne Moon-Crane. Daphne's pregnancy is revealed in the eleventh season premiere,No Sex Please, We're Skittish. In the series finale, Daphne gives birth to David at veterinarian clinic on the same day his grandfather,Martin Crane, marriesRonee Lawrence. David comes back in theFrasier reboot. David is born on May 15, 2004 - on the same dayMartinis to wedRonee Lawrenceand Eddie’s birthday. While at the venue where the wedding will take place,Eddieaccidentally eats the wedding rings, resulting in Niles and Daphne having to take him to the vet. Once there, Daphne's water breaks and she goes into labor. Upon receiving the news, Frasier, Martin and Ronee arrive at the vet to meet David and fall in love with him instantly. Ronee then suggests that she and Martin get married at the clinic, so Daphne and Niles do not miss out, and Frasier marries them on the spot.

characters.name: David Moon
characters.description: David Moonis a one-shot character onFrasier,and is a member of the Moon family. He is the son ofHarryandGertrude Moon, and the brother ofDaphne Moon. He is briefly seen (unnamed) in "Something Borrowed, Someone Blue", when a group of Daphne's brothers move the pre-wedding party into the room Daphne and Niles are in, where the duo are discussing their feelings for each other. The actor playing the role is not identified on IMDb.

characters.name: Deb Chesterton
characters.description: Deb ChestertonBornDeborah (last name unknown)OccupationOwner at Auto Body Repair ShopRelationshipsRomancesGil Chesterton(husband)BackstageDeb Chestertonis an unseen character on NBC series Frasier. Deb is the unseen wife ofGil Chesterton. She is supposedly a graduate of Sarah Lawrence and owns a very successful auto body repair shop. She is brought up in retaliation to Roz, Frasier, and Bulldog's insinuation that Gil is gay, and Roz mentions that she always believed Deb was just his cat. Deb is the unseen wife ofGil Chesterton. characters.name: Denise Doyle
characters.description: Denise DoyleRelationshipsFamilyRoz Doyle(sister)Joanna Doyle(mother)Alice Doyle(niece)RomancesFrasier Crane(one date)Craig(ex-husband)BackstagePortrayed bySuzanne CryerSeason(s)Season 9•Season 11First seenLast seenThe Guilt TrippersSeaBee JeebiesEpisodes2Denise Doyleis a minor character onFrasier, portrayed by Suzanne Cryer. Denise is the competitive sister toRoz Doyle, and daughter ofJoanna Doyle. She is initially married but her husband later leaves her (offscreen). She isFrasier's date to the 2004 SeaBea Award Ceremony. Denise is the competitive sister toRoz Doyle, and daughter ofJoanna Doyle. She is initially married but her husband later leaves her (offscreen). She isFrasier's date to the 2004 SeaBea Award Ceremony.

characters.name: Derek Mann
characters.description: Derek MannBackstageFirst seenLast seenI Hate Frasier CraneThe Botched Language Of Cranes(mentioned)Derek Mannis a guest character onFrasier. He is voiced by Joe Mantegna. Mann is a Seattle columnist who writes an article entitled "I Hate Frasier Crane" - which was in fact the entirety of the article.Frasiercomplains over the air that his prose lacks substance, and that he had no good reason behind his statement. Mann gets angry and challenges Frasier to a physical fight on the air. Frasier ultimately decides to go through with it, but the police break up the fight before it can begin. characters.name: Diane Chambers
characters.description: Diane ChambersAliasWhiteyResidenceBoston, MAOccupationWaiter • WriterRelationshipsFamilySpencer Chambers(father)Helen Chambers(mother)Elizabeth †(cat)RomancesSam Malone(ex-boyfriend)Frasier Crane(ex-fiancée)BackstagePortrayed byShelley LongSeason(s)2•3•9First seenLast seenAdventures In Paradise: Part 2Don Juan In Hell: Part 2Episodes3 Diane Chambersis a guest character onFrasier, portrayed byShelley Long. Diane is a major character on the sitcomCheersand a 'visitor' onFrasier. She was created for and primarily appeared onCheers, for its first five seasons. She was a well-educated writer who became, of necessity, a barmaid at Cheers. She soon was wooed bySam Malone, but later began dating the psychiatristDr. Frasier Crane. However, she eventually left Frasier at the altar and dated Sam again. She left after that, and returned only in the finale of the series.

characters.name: Donny Douglas
characters.description: Donny DouglasBornDonald Ronald DouglasAliasThe PiranhaOccupationDivorce attorneyRelationshipsRomancesBridgett(fiancee or ex fiancee or wife. Left open)Nancy(ex-wife)Daphne Moon(ex-fiancée)Roz Doyle(ex-girlfriend)BackstagePortrayed bySaul RubinekSeason(s)6,7,8,10First seenLast seenTo Tell The TruthThe Ring CycleEpisodes15 Nancy(ex-wife)Daphne Moon(ex-fiancée)Roz Doyle(ex-girlfriend) Donny Douglasis a recurring character on NBC seriesFrasier. He is portrayed bySaul Rubinek. Donny is an unorthodox but extremely successful attorney. He first datedRoz Doyle(prior to the show), before he was hired, on her recommendation, as the divorce lawyer forNiles Crane. While working on Niles' case, he becomes acquainted withDaphne Moon, to whom he gets engaged. Donny isn't at first the expectation of Niles and Frasier, but his bold attitude changes their mind. His nickname in legal circles is 'the Piranha', although Roz deflates this by explaining that he has a 'night-grinding problem' with regard to his teeth. Donny successfully wins Niles a large divorce settlement without the need to go to court by unearthing the source of Maris' family fortune—not timber, as she has always claimed, but urinal cakes. After this, he begins seeing Daphne. He is eventually left at the altar by Daphne, in favor of Niles. He becomes sour after being abandoned, and files a lawsuit against both Daphne andFrasier, the latter of whom he holds responsible for advising Daphne to forsake him. He later drops both lawsuits. Donny eventually marriesa womanhe knew for only two weeks. In "The Ring Cycle", he meets Daphne and Niles when he is about to marry another woman. It then becomes clear that he is still not over Daphne leaving him. He received his law degree from the University of Las Vegas, a fact which provokes barely masked derision from Niles when he first learns it.

characters.description: DukeResidenceSeattleOccupation1954—1994Owner atDuke'sRelationshipsFamilyMarie (daughter)FriendsMartin CraneBackstagePortrayed byJohn LaMottaSeason(s)2•5First seenLast seenDuke's, We Hardly Knew YeWhere Every Bloke Knows Your NameEpisodes2Dukeis a character onFrasier, portrayed by John LaMotta. characters.name: Eddie
characters.description: Eddie CraneBirthday15 MayAliasEddie SpaghettiResidenceFrasier's ApartmentOccupationPetRelationshipsFamilyMartin Crane(owner)6 puppiesRomancesPhoebeBackstagePortrayed byMoseEnzo Eddieis a character onFrasier, portrayed by Moose for the first seven seasons, and by his son, Enzo, for the remaining four. Eddie is the Jack Russell Terrier owned byMartin Crane. He is capable of understanding many commands and Human language to a certain degree, for instance on display when someone spells out B-A-T-H and Eddie scarpers to avoid it. Eddie has a tendency to, at length, sit and stare atFrasier. After about a year living inFrasier's Apartment, Eddie fathers six puppies, which causes Frasier to have him neutered.[1]His puppies also have a tendency to stare at Frasier. He generally listens only to Martin, and has an inconsistency in learning tricks. He occasionally listens toDaphne Moon, and only fully listens toLilith Sterninout of fright during her visits. Despite his complicated relationship with Frasier he is shown on frequent occasions to listen to Frasier, albeit out fear when he is frustrated.
